name: university-ai
description: University AI/ML course helper: study plans, concept explanations, problem sets, project guidance, exam prep for ML/DL/RL courses.
author: anwar

commands.name: ai:concept
commands.description: Explain AI/ML concept with intuition and math
commands.prompt:
Explain {{concept}} for a university {{level}} student. Include:
1. Intuition: real-world analogy, visual description
2. Mathematical formulation: equations, derivations, assumptions
3. Algorithmic perspective: pseudocode, complexity
4. Implementation: PyTorch/NumPy/JAX code snippet
5. Common pitfalls and misconceptions
6. Applications: where it's used, why it works
7. Connections: related concepts, prerequisites, extensions
8. Practice problems: 2-3 with hints
9. Further reading: textbook chapters, papers, videos

Concept: {{concept}} (e.g., backpropagation, attention, VAE, policy gradient)
Level: {{level}} (undergrad, grad, phd)
Course: {{course}} (e.g., CS231n, CS224n, 6.S191, MLSS)

commands.name: ai:study-plan
commands.description: Create personalized study plan for AI course
commands.prompt:
Create a {{duration}}-week study plan for {{course}}. Student background: {{background}}.

Include:
1. Weekly breakdown: topics, readings, lectures, assignments
2. Prerequisites check: linear algebra, calculus, probability, Python
3. Resources: textbooks, lecture videos (YouTube/MIT/Stanford), papers
4. Hands-on: coding assignments, Kaggle, Colab notebooks
5. Milestones: concept mastery, project checkpoints, exam prep
6. Time allocation: lectures, reading, coding, review per week
7. Accountability: study group, progress tracking, office hours
8. Exam prep: past exams, cheat sheet, practice problems
9. Project ideas: course project, portfolio, research

Course: {{course}} (e.g., CS229, CS231n, CS224n, FAI, DLSS, RL)
Duration: {{duration}} weeks
Background: {{background}}

commands.name: ai:problem-set
commands.prompt:
Generate {{num_problems}} practice problems for {{topic}} at {{level}} level.

For each problem:
1. Problem statement: clear, self-contained, realistic
2. Difficulty: easy/medium/hard
3. Concepts tested
4. Hints: progressive, without giving away
5. Full solution: math derivation, code, explanation
6. Variations: easier/harder extensions
7. Common mistakes
8. Time estimate

Topic: {{topic}} (e.g., CNN, Transformer, RL, GAN, VAE, Optimization)
Level: {{level}} (intro, intermediate, advanced)
Number: {{num_problems}}

commands.name: ai:project
commands.prompt:
Guide a course project for {{course}} on {{topic}}.

Provide:
1. Project ideas: 3-5 with scope, novelty, feasibility
2. Project structure: proposal, milestones, final report
3. Baseline implementation: starter code, data, evaluation
4. Extension ideas: improvements, ablations, analysis
5. Literature: 5-10 key papers to read
6. Evaluation: metrics, baselines, statistical significance
7. Write-up: structure, figures, tables, reproducibility
8. Presentation: slides, demo, Q&A prep
9. Grading rubric alignment
10. Portfolio: GitHub, blog, video

Course: {{course}}
Topic: {{topic}}
Team size: {{team_size}}
Duration: {{duration}} weeks

commands.name: ai:exam-prep
commands.prompt:
Create exam prep for {{course}} {{exam_type}}.

Include:
1. Topic checklist: all concepts with mastery levels
2. Formula sheet: key equations, theorems, algorithms
3. Concept maps: connections between topics
4. Practice questions: multiple choice, short answer, derivation, coding
5. Past exam analysis: patterns, frequency, difficulty
6. Study schedule: {{days_until}} days remaining
7. Active recall: flashcards (Anki format), self-testing
8. Weak area identification and remediation
9. Exam day strategy: time allocation, question order
10. Stress management: sleep, nutrition, anxiety

Course: {{course}}
Exam type: {{exam_type}} (midterm, final, qualifying)
Days until: {{days_until}}

agents.name: ai-tutor
agents.prompt: You are an expert AI/ML tutor for university courses. Cover: ML theory (bias-variance, PAC, VC-dim), optimization (SGD, Adam, second-order), DL (CNN, RNN, Transformer, GNN), Generative (VAE, GAN, Diffusion, Flow), RL (MDP, PG, Actor-Critic, Q-learning), Probabilistic (HMM, Kalman, VI, MCMC). Teaching style: Feynman technique, visual intuition, mathematical rigor, code implementation, research connections. Provide: concept explanations, study plans, problem sets, project guidance, exam prep, paper reading, research advice.
